labels.rationale: Evidence: latent states are permutation invariant. Public asset: ADNI plus LNODE code. Metric: reconstruction MSE and latent-space silhouette. Hypothesis: ordering regularization should make disease-stage latents more separable without hurting PET reconstruction. The idea is scientifically clear, but ADNI access/preprocessing and the exact runnable LNODE reproduction are not guaranteed in 24-72h.
labels.repair_needed: Provide an immediately downloadable preprocessed ADNI regional SUVR split, or a verified artifact command that reproduces the baseline without new data-access approval.

labels.rationale: Evidence: trade-off between legible meaning and detection avoidance. Public asset: Jigsaw Civil Comments Kaggle data. Metric: legibility-detection trade-off/AUTC or LDR. Baseline: TextFooler-style adversarial attacks. Hypothesis: substitution density has a measurable point where legibility falls faster than evasion improves. The 1000-sample transformation sweep is executable quickly.

Evidence: multi-seed full-scale runs are future work. Public asset: crc-screen repository. Metric is only 'pre-registered benchmark delta' and the hypothesis/intervention repeat a template rather than defining what changes in the CRC-Screen protocol. Baseline is named but not operationalized for the proposed ablation.
labels.repair_needed: Replace the template with a concrete multi-seed protocol, exact seed count, metric, split, and compute budget.
labels.fatal_blocker: Missing specific intervention, specific metric, and falsifiable hypothesis.

labels.rationale: Evidence: low cross-source overlap and many single-source genes. Public asset: PhenotypeToGeneDownloaderR repository plus curated known phenotype-associated genes/raw retrievals described in the input. Metric: MAP and Precision@K. Baseline: unweighted source-frequency ranking. Hypothesis: source-precision weighting improves MAP. The weighting rerank can be run as a bounded offline analysis.

Evidence: UniPrefill focuses on prefill rather than decoding. Public asset: UniPrefill code and RULER. Metric: ITL/PPL or decoding TPS. Baseline: standard vLLM decoding. Hypothesis: carrying token-drop masks into decoding reduces latency with little quality loss. The proposed Triton/vLLM masked-KV change is too implementation-heavy for a reliable 24-72h run.
labels.repair_needed: Define a no-kernel pilot, such as offline masked-attention simulation or existing-kernel ablation, with one model, one prompt set, and exact latency/quality measurement.

labels.rationale: Evidence: robustness to adversarial annotations is future work. Public asset: crc-screen repository. Metric is generic benchmark delta; baseline is only the reported protocol; hypothesis and intervention are templated and do not define adversarial annotation generation or evaluation. This is not yet a runnable experiment.
labels.repair_needed: Specify the adversarial annotation transformations, calibration/test split, certification metric, and comparison point.
labels.fatal_blocker: Missing concrete intervention, metric, and executable 24-72h protocol.

labels.rationale: Evidence: ToM helps stylized cooperation but shows near-zero/negative AI-for-Science outcome effects. Public asset: agent-cooperation code/data and Zenodo task data. Metric: completion rate and token overhead. Baseline: standard instruction prompting. Hypothesis: ToM prompting adds overhead that hurts realistic time-constrained workflows. The proposed two-agent task comparison is bounded and runnable.

Evidence: RULER accuracy drops from 64K to 128K. Public asset: UniPrefill code and RULER. Metric: 128K RULER accuracy. Baseline: default UniPrefill top-p. Hypothesis: dynamic sparsity thresholds preserve long-context accuracy better than fixed top-p. The design is clear, but full 128K RULER sensitivity runs may exceed a dependable 24-72h path without a specified model/hardware budget.
labels.repair_needed: Constrain the pilot to a named small model, one RULER subset, exact context lengths, and available hardware or a reduced-context proxy that still tests the threshold mechanism.

labels.rationale: Evidence: sequence similarity fails under taxonomic-family holdout. Public asset: crc-screen repository. Metric, hypothesis, and intervention are generic template text rather than an actual ablation of the failure mode. Baseline is named but no comparison protocol is defined.
labels.repair_needed: Define a concrete family-holdout experiment comparing sequence similarity against annotation/text signals with certified miss-rate and false-positive metrics.
labels.fatal_blocker: Missing specific intervention and falsifiable metric.

labels.rationale: Evidence: clock synchronization would enable absolute ToF and finer ranging/SLAM. Public asset: 60GHz-RFSoC-Dataset. Metric: distance MAE. Baseline: RSS trilateration from beamspace intensity maps. Hypothesis: chirp correlation can recover stable relative timing for decimeter ranging even without hardware sync. The single-LoS cross-correlation pilot is concrete and fast.

Evidence: rare-disease diagnosis models fail to distinguish common from rare disease. Public asset: RareArena/Hygieia. Metric: common-vs-rare macro-F1 plus diagnostic accuracy. Baseline: zero-shot LLM prompting. Hypothesis: a phenotype-embedding case router reduces common-disease misdiagnosis. The rare side is public, but the common-disease comparator dataset is unspecified.
labels.repair_needed: Name one public common-disease case dataset and provide the exact sampling/mapping procedure for the mixed evaluation set.

labels.rationale: Evidence is only that seven limitations exist. Public asset is not specified with a URL in this candidate. Metric and hypothesis are generic benchmark-delta template text, and the intervention is not defined. There is no falsifiable 24-72h experiment.
labels.repair_needed: Choose one named limitation, identify the repository/data artifact, and define a concrete measurement against the reported baseline.
labels.fatal_blocker: Missing specific problem, artifact, metric, and intervention.

labels.rationale: Evidence: FairEnc preserves fairness but AUC does not fully generalize to FairFundus. Public asset: FairEnc code and publicly described Harvard-FairVLMed/FairFundus evaluation setting. Metric: AUC and fairness metrics. Baseline: original FairEnc. Hypothesis: domain-invariant learning improves the performance-fairness trade-off under domain shift. Baseline replication plus linear-probe evaluation is bounded.

Evidence: model randomness causes inconsistent rare-disease diagnosis outputs. Public asset: MyGene2 cases and Hygieia code. Metric: diagnostic stability/agreement rate and coefficient of variation. Baseline: single-pass LLM inference. Hypothesis: verifier-corrector loops reduce output variance. Re-running 20 public cases over multiple seeds is an immediate executable pilot.

Evidence is a broad future-work statement about antiviral repurposing platforms. Public assets are listed, but the intervention is templated, metric is generic benchmark delta, and the scientific hypothesis is not a mechanism. This cannot be independently run as written.
labels.repair_needed: Define a specific antiviral-drug-discovery model/tool comparison, target dataset, endpoint, and baseline.
labels.fatal_blocker: Missing concrete intervention and falsifiable hypothesis.

labels.rationale: Evidence: FairEnc requires careful hyperparameter tuning to balance performance and fairness. Public asset: FairEnc code and Harvard-FairVLMed. Metric: AUC-DPD Pareto hypervolume/ES-AUC. Baseline: manually tuned Table 2 hyperparameters. Hypothesis: automated multi-objective search finds a better Pareto front. A small Optuna integration and search-space definition is executable within the window.

Evidence: dense bmDCA over-parameterization can exceed training-set information. Public asset: Pfam PF00425 plus entropy-sparse-DCA/highentropyDCA/adabmDCA code. Metric: held-out log-likelihood and entropy/sequence-space volume. Baseline: fully connected bmDCA. Hypothesis: entropy-guided sparsification improves generalization and expands functional sequence space. The MSA split and edge-decimation pilot is directly runnable.

Evidence repeats a broad future-work statement on antiviral repurposing/design platforms. Public assets exist, but metric, hypothesis, and intervention remain generic template text. The candidate lacks a specific experimental manipulation.
labels.repair_needed: Specify the antiviral benchmark task, model/tool change, metric such as binding-affinity error or docking success, and reported baseline.
labels.fatal_blocker: Missing concrete intervention and measurable hypothesis.

labels.rationale: Evidence: judge-dependence suggests behavioral vs content-policy constructs differ. Public asset: OLMo-3 open weights/datasets are named, but the released protocol URL is absent here. Metric: inter-judge agreement/effect size. Baseline: primary judge results. Hypothesis: eval-cautious behavior depends on alignment between post-training data and judge rubric. The first-24h plan mostly replicates scoring divergence, not the training-data mechanism.
labels.repair_needed: Provide the exact released protocol/artifact URL and a small measurable proxy for judge-rubric/training-data alignment that the pilot tests directly.

labels.rationale: Evidence only says several limitations are immediate. Public asset: EML biophysics repository. Metric is generic benchmark delta and the intervention is a template string. The hypothesis is not falsifiable beyond 'something changes versus baseline.'
labels.repair_needed: Choose one specific EML limitation and define a held-out predictive-error experiment with exact model depth and dataset.
labels.fatal_blocker: Missing specific limitation, intervention, and metric.

labels.rationale: Evidence: OLMo-3 shows post-training inversion of a deployment-cautious base prior. Public asset: OLMo code/open assets. Metric: H3 interaction beta and correlation with evaluation-framed data density. Baseline: original OLMo-3-Instruct ECD effect. Hypothesis: test-like SFT/RLHF examples cause the inversion. The fine-tuning plan lacks an exact public dataset slice and prompt set for a 24-72h run.
labels.repair_needed: Name the alignment/SFT subset, define how evaluation-framed examples are identified or synthesized, and provide the exact ECD prompt set.

labels.rationale: Evidence is a broad statement about usual constraints on reduced dynamical modeling. Public asset: EML repository. Metric and hypothesis are generic template fields, and 'stress-test and mitigate' is not a concrete intervention. Baseline is not enough to make it runnable.
labels.repair_needed: Define one stressor, one mitigation, and one held-out prediction metric for the EML benchmark.
labels.fatal_blocker: Missing specific intervention and falsifiable endpoint.

labels.rationale: Evidence: tool-integrated reasoning needs benchmarks that distinguish reasoning from tool-call errors, but the evidence span is only title-level. Public asset: ToolBench. Metric: Tool Call Accuracy/Pass@1. Baseline: direct tool-calling SFT. Hypothesis: explicit thinking traces reduce hallucinated arguments. The plan assumes usable thought-trace training data and fine-tunes Llama-3-8B, which is not yet a tight 24-72h path.
labels.repair_needed: Use an existing trace-bearing public subset or convert the pilot to inference-only prompting with exact ToolBench examples and argument-error scoring.

labels.rationale: Evidence: future work on calibration data, time-varying fields, and amyloid plaque formation. Public asset is only 'public benchmark related to the paper' with no repository or dataset URL. Metric and hypothesis are generic template text. No executable baseline is identified.
labels.repair_needed: Provide a public imaging/tracer dataset or code artifact plus one concrete extension such as time-varying parameter fields and a reconstruction metric.
labels.fatal_blocker: Missing public artifact, metric specificity, and 24-72h runnable path.

labels.rationale: Evidence: experiments expose possible cross-artifact state drift but do not measure multi-round accumulation. Public asset: GAIA is named, but it is not a direct benchmark for policy-memo artifact consistency. Metric: State Drift Index/cross-artifact consistency. Baseline: ReAct loop. Hypothesis: DAG externalization reduces drift. Plausible, but the task construction is synthetic and weakly grounded in a public artifact.
labels.repair_needed: Replace GAIA with the paper's controlled policy-memo tasks or release a fixed multi-round artifact-update benchmark with gold dependencies.

labels.rationale: Evidence is a theoretical PCBF inequality failure condition. Public asset is unspecified. Metric and hypothesis are generic benchmark-delta template text, and the intervention is 'stress-test and mitigate' without a concrete system or simulator. There is no runnable public benchmark.
labels.repair_needed: Identify a simulator/artifact, instantiate the inequality parameters, and define a stability/obfuscation violation metric.
labels.fatal_blocker: Missing public artifact and executable experiment.

labels.rationale: Evidence and feasibility spans are only 'Recursive Agent Optimization.' Public asset: WebShop. Metric: task success rate. Baseline: ReAct. Hypothesis: recursive self-optimization improves web navigation. It is runnable in principle, but the scientific value is weak/crowded and the mechanism is under-specified by the evidence.
labels.repair_needed: Define the recursive optimizer algorithm, stopping rule, prompt-update objective, and a stronger mechanism-specific endpoint beyond WebShop success rate.

labels.rationale: Evidence: two bounds compose by a union-bound argument. Public asset is unspecified. Metric, hypothesis, and intervention are generic template text, and the cited statement is not itself a failure mode needing an ablation. No concrete baseline execution is available.
labels.repair_needed: Define an implemented PCBF update cycle, a failure-probability estimator, and a baseline condition to compare.
labels.fatal_blocker: Missing public artifact, intervention, and falsifiable endpoint.

labels.rationale: Evidence: return alone hides coordination failures such as conflicts. Public asset: STAT environment and executable MARL code. Metric: conflict rate. Baseline: QMIX implementation in the artifact. Hypothesis: transformer-based MARL reduces conflicts versus monotonic mixing at high agent counts. The first step is a concrete repo baseline run on a named configuration.

Evidence: future datasets may include multi-transmitter, multi-user, and dynamic measurements. Public asset is described only generically with no URL in this candidate. Metric and hypothesis are generic template text, and the future data do not currently exist in the supplied asset. Not executable in 24-72h.
labels.repair_needed: Use an existing public dynamic/multi-transmitter dataset or redefine the experiment as a simulation with exact channel model and metric.
labels.fatal_blocker: Proposed data extension is absent; no current public artifact supports the experiment.

labels.rationale: Evidence: IQL has high conflict rate and limited coordination in interdependent settings. Public asset: STAT coordination-aware MARL repository. Metric: conflict rate. Baseline: vanilla IQL. Hypothesis: local communication of task targets reduces conflicts toward CTDE levels. The observation-space modification and short training pilot are concrete.

Evidence: optical sensors fail in smoke/dust/steam. Public asset is only a generic mmWave dataset reference; the candidate lacks a smoke/dust/steam benchmark, concrete intervention, and real metric beyond template benchmark delta. The failure mode is not testable with the supplied dataset alone.
labels.repair_needed: Provide a paired optical/mmWave degraded-visibility benchmark or a validated simulation protocol with localization/reconstruction metrics.
labels.fatal_blocker: Missing relevant public data for the claimed failure mode.

labels.rationale: Evidence: generated proposals can contain internal contradictions or unsupported mechanisms. Public asset: Evolving-Idea-Graphs code plus AI Idea Bench/LiveIdeaBench resources. Metric: internal consistency score and novelty/feasibility. Baseline: original EIG schema. Hypothesis: counterfactual nodes reduce contradictions. The 10-sample schema modification pilot is bounded and measurable.

Evidence: robustness checks across random seeds and train/validation splits are needed. Public asset: actuarial repository. Metric is only generic benchmark delta, while the hypothesis is templated. Baseline: reported longevity protocol. The idea is worthwhile, but the exact stability endpoint and split/seed plan are missing.
labels.repair_needed: Specify the forecast metric, countries, number of seeds/splits, confidence interval target, and pass/fail threshold.

labels.rationale: Evidence: graph critic trained from heuristic weak labels may inherit teacher bias. Public asset: idea-graph code plus LiveIdeaBench/AI Idea Bench resources. Metric: expert rating and benchmark novelty/feasibility. Baseline: heuristic-trained EIG critic. Hypothesis: expert-ranked traces improve evaluator alignment. The first-24h plan depends on collecting 50 expert rankings, which is not a public 24-72h asset.
labels.repair_needed: Replace expert collection with an existing public annotation/evaluator proxy, or pre-provide the 50 expert-ranked traces.

labels.rationale: Evidence only says the research opens several avenues for extension. Public asset: actuarial repository. Metric, hypothesis, and intervention are generic template text and do not identify any concrete extension. Baseline is named but no experiment is defined.
labels.repair_needed: Choose a specific extension and metric, such as cross-country dispersion modeling or seed robustness, with exact data and protocol.
labels.fatal_blocker: Missing specific scientific hypothesis and intervention.

labels.rationale: Evidence: actuarial-informed neural networks could embed Li-Lee coherence, Gompertz monotonicity, and stationarity constraints in the loss. Public asset: actuarial repository. Baseline: reported neural-actuarial protocol. Metric is generic benchmark delta and the current intervention is still template text. The direction is concrete enough to track but not an A without a specified constraint/loss and endpoint.
labels.repair_needed: Define one constraint term, the exact loss formula, forecast metric, countries, and comparison against the current anchored LSTM.

labels.rationale: Evidence: most platforms skip requirement elicitation and IQS varies widely. Public asset: SWE-WebDevBench repository with prompts, canary requirements, and judges. Metric: Canary Retention Rate. Baseline: direct generation from the initial prompt. Hypothesis: multi-turn elicitation improves canary retention. The P1 prompt baseline and elicitation-loop comparison are directly executable.

Evidence is a high-level statement that extensions would strengthen theoretical foundations. Public asset: actuarial repository. Metric, hypothesis, and intervention are generic template text with no concrete extension or falsifiable endpoint. This is not a runnable candidate.
labels.repair_needed: Translate the theoretical-extension claim into one concrete actuarial constraint or robustness experiment with a named metric.
labels.fatal_blocker: Missing concrete intervention and measurable hypothesis.

notes: Applied a strict A threshold: public artifact/data, concrete metric, baseline, scientific hypothesis, and a credible 24-72h execution path all had to be present.
Rejected generic auto-template candidates where the intervention was 'design a paper-specific ablation' and the metric was only 'pre-registered benchmark delta'.
Assigned B where the scientific direction was promising but one concrete repair was needed, usually exact data access, protocol, compute scope, or metric definition.
Assigned C where the idea was plausible and somewhat runnable but weakly grounded, crowded, or measured by an ad hoc synthetic benchmark.
